title: [release-1.11] Bump Go Jose to v3.0.5, CVE-2026-34986, Skopeo to v1.11.5
body:
Bump Go Jose to v3.0.5 to address CVE-2026-34986

Fixes: https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-81778,
https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-81771,
https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-81767,
https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-81764,
https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHEL-164982, https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHEL-164980

comments.body: @mtrmac I'm not sure what the fix is for the CI?  Do you have a pointer?

@TomSweeneyRedHat first question, does this _need_ to bump the Go version? The `go-jose` package did not bump its Go version requirement, and in a minimal test, bumping the dependency + `go mod tidy -compat=1.17` seems to work without moving to Go 1.20.

---

CI runs against images created by https://github.com/containers/automation_images . So, this branch is running against a 2022 image.

The first question would be what is our target platform / version and whether we can easily have that existing in public CI (with CentOS stream a permanently moving target).

I’m not _very_ familiar with the automation_images repo but I assume that creating an image targeting an old operating system and old Go version might be non-trivial. (Or maybe it would be easy enough, just choosing a base Fedora tag?? If we have a system to target, that is.) At least, that repo does not contain any stable branches itself.

Other options include force-merging and worrying about it later, or removing the CI entirely and relying on $some manual replacement.

Thanks @mtrmac for all the info.  I don't recall having changed the Go version myself, but perhaps I did.  Regardless, I've popped it back to 1.17 and we'll see if that cures the fun.  It did compile on my machine, so fingers crossed.

```
vendor/github.com/go-jose/go-jose/v3/symmetric.go:373:16: undefined: bytes.Clone
```

Doh, and I now see the history of the PR showing that you did run into this and fixed it by updating the Go version. I’m sorry about the repeated work.

So we are still at “our images are too old to support this code”. What is the right thing to do about that on this branch?

I dug deeper today, and Go 1.20 first introduced bytes.Clone(), and you're right, Go Jose is still using go 1.12 in their go.mod file for v3.  I'm not sure how they managed that.  Let me go dig further as I'm just getting back to this after chasing other fires.

I'll poke at this more tomorrow. I couldn't get the vendoring to work without running into the 

```
# github.com/go-jose/go-jose/v3
vendor/github.com/go-jose/go-jose/v3/symmetric.go:373:16: undefined: bytes.Clone
make: *** [Makefile:239: validate-local] Error 2
```

So I chickened out and pushed it through.  I'll see if I can figure out the CI angle next.
